judges.reasoning: The submission delivers an exceptionally polished WebGL lava lamp with complex physics and metaball rendering that perfectly captures the requested behavior. It goes above and beyond with realistic glass reflections, volumetric lighting, and a pre-warmed simulation state so the action starts immediately.

Render frames show a polished lamp with organic metaball wax that deforms, merges, and cycles from bottom heat to top cool with hypnotic pacing. Fully self-contained, meets every requirement, and exceeds with WebGL shading, vessel chrome, and interactive stir.

The submission uses a sophisticated WebGL metaball shader with custom physics for heat conduction, buoyancy, and deformation, complete with a Canvas2D fallback. The visual styling and realistic pacing are exceptionally well-executed and mesmerizing.

The submission is a single self-contained HTML file with no external dependencies, and its WebGL metaball renderer plus Canvas2D fallback should run flawlessly on first open in modern browsers. It delivers the requested physics—heating, rising, cooling, sinking, merging, and deforming blobs—inside a polished CSS lamp housing with lighting, rim glow, and interactive stirring, going clearly beyond the basic ask.

Self-contained single file with comprehensive WebGL metaball shader producing smooth, deforming, merging wax blobs plus a Canvas2D fallback. Physics is rich (per-blob heat/buoyancy, adhesion that melts with temperature, drifting bulb hotspot, lane-based depth, wall/floor/ceiling cling) and includes a warm-up loop so action starts immediately. Aesthetic is polished with cap/base/halo/seam CSS, lighting, rim, streaks, and click-to-stir interaction.
